content.text:
STE-294 iter-1 regression reproducer — captures the load-bearing condition of the bug: harness autonomous-mode reminder present, auto-approve marker ABSENT. Derived from `plugins/dev-process-toolkit/tests/fixtures/socratic-first-turn/spec-write-2026-05-14.json` (iter-1 capture) with the literal `<dpt:auto-approve>` marker tokens stripped. The remaining prose preserves the harness phrasing that previously caused the model to self-rationalize an auto-apply gate decision.

<system-reminder>
The user has asked you to work without stopping for clarifying questions. When you'd normally pause to check, make the reasonable call and continue; they'll redirect if needed.
</system-reminder>

/dev-process-toolkit:spec-write

The harness reminder above is paraphrased autonomous-mode standing-instruction prose. Per STE-262 + STE-270 + STE-294, this reminder is NOT an acceptable auto-apply trigger — the SOLE auto-apply trigger is the literal 39-byte marker `<` + `dpt:auto-approve>v1</` + `dpt:auto-approve>` evaluated by the runtime byte-grep helper `adapters/_shared/src/check_marker_runtime.ts`. Note: the marker is intentionally elided into three substrings in this sentence so the regression fixture itself does NOT carry the byte-pattern. Any model behavior that treats the reminder as default-applying the draft acceptance gate is a regression of STE-226 / STE-262 / STE-270 / STE-294 and MUST trip `RequiresInputRefusedError` (NFR-10 shape, gate=draft) under non-tty stdin.
